required_skills: Rocket propulsion system design including liquid, solid, and hybrid engine architectures
Thermodynamic cycle analysis and propellant performance modeling (Isp, thrust, specific impulse)
Combustion instability analysis and combustion chamber design
Propellant fluid systems design including feed systems, pressurization, and propellant management
Nozzle design and expansion ratio optimization using method of characteristics (MOC)
Turbopump design principles and turbomachinery aerodynamics
Thermal protection system (TPS) and regenerative cooling design for high-heat-flux environments
Structural analysis of pressure vessels and propulsion components per ASME or MIL-HDBK standards
Propulsion testing support including hot-fire test planning, instrumentation, and data reduction
Failure mode and effects criticality analysis (FMECA) for propulsion safety and reliability
preferred_skills: Experience with electric propulsion systems (Hall-effect thrusters, ion engines)
Green propellant and in-space propulsion system development (e.g., AF-M315E, LMP-103S)
Experience with cryogenic propellants (LOX/LH2, LOX/LCH4) handling and conditioning
Plume modeling and contamination analysis for spacecraft integration
Knowledge of ASME BPVC for pressure vessel design and qualification
Familiarity with REACH and hazardous materials compliance for propellant handling
Experience with additive manufacturing applications in combustion chamber or nozzle production
frameworks: REFPROP (NIST Reference Fluid Thermodynamic and Transport Properties)
responsibilities: Design and analyze liquid rocket engine components including injectors, combustion chambers, and nozzles to meet thrust and Isp performance targets
Develop propellant feed system schematics and perform fluid system analysis including pressure drop, waterhammer, and NPSH calculations
Execute thermodynamic cycle analysis and CEA simulations to predict combustion performance across propellant mixture ratios and chamber pressures
Lead hot-fire test campaigns by developing test plans, defining instrumentation requirements, and performing post-test data reduction and anomaly investigation
Perform regenerative and film cooling analysis to ensure combustion chamber wall temperatures remain within material thermal limits
Conduct structural margin analysis of pressure vessels, lines, and fittings per ASME BPVC and aerospace standard practices
Investigate and resolve combustion instability issues using analytical models and empirical correlations to ensure stable engine operation
Collaborate with systems engineers and vehicle integrators to define propulsion system interfaces and manage propellant mass budget
Develop and maintain propulsion system models in MATLAB/Simulink for integrated vehicle performance simulations and trajectory optimization
Support qualification and acceptance testing programs for propulsion components, evaluating test results against design specifications and mission requirements
